## 1. Macro / Rates / Inflation

**HIGH**: Fed meeting March 17-18; decision March 18. Widely expected to hold at 3.5%-3.75%. Energy price spike from Iran war raises inflation concerns despite recent 2.4% CPI print (Feb). **MEDIUM**: CPI (Feb 2026): +0.3% MoM, +2.4% YoY. Core PCE for Jan expected 3.1% (released 3/13). Core PCE nowcast 3.1% represents 0.1pp rise from Dec. Sticky inflation backdrop.

**MEDIUM**: Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% Jan 28. Powell's term ends May 2026; Kevin Warsh nominated. Chair transition adds volatility premium.

**Signal interaction**: CONFIRMS precious_metals, monetary_hedges sleeve strength. CONTRADICTS rate-cut trades.

## 3. AI / Semiconductor

**MEDIUM**: US allows H200, MI325X exports to China on case-by-case basis with 25% tariff (announced Dec 2025, implemented early 2026). Security requirements + performance testing mandated. Nvidia CFO: H200 approved for China but "yet to generate any revenue." China accounted for 1/5 of Nvidia data center revenue historically. Export restrictions still binding; China pivot to domestic alternatives (Huawei Ascend).

**LOW**: Trump admin withdrew global licensing system for AI chip exports (draft rule pulled). Core export controls remain; China restrictions unchanged. Relief for international sales ex-China.

**MEDIUM**: FTC advancing broad antitrust probe into Microsoft (cloud, AI, software). Amazon, Apple litigation ongoing. Regulatory pressure on Big Tech continues but non-binding near-term.

**Signal interaction**: CONFIRMS ai_tech cautiously. Export controls are headwind but infrastructure demand (data centers, AI capex) remains structural tailwind. No immediate override trigger.

## 4. Energy / Materials

**MEDIUM**: March 2026 Nuclear Energy Summit (France) formalizes commitment to triple nuclear capacity by 2050. Policy coordination strengthens uranium demand outlook. Spot uranium rose 25% in Jan 2026, surpassing $100/lb (corrected to $89). US DoE awarded $2.7B contracts Jan 2026 to rebuild domestic enrichment capacity. Jurisdictional advantage for US/Canada/Australia producers.

**MEDIUM**: Small modular reactors (SMR) gaining traction for AI/data center baseload power. Structural uranium demand driver beyond traditional utility procurement.

**Signal interaction**: CONFIRMS strategic_materials (URNM). Uranium policy tailwinds structural, not speculative. Long-cycle supply constraints support multi-year bull case.

## 6. Biotech / FDA

**HIGH**: TransCon CNP PDUFA Feb 28, 2026 (navepegritide, achondroplasia). FDA approved Feb 27. XBI posted 2.8% gain in Jan 2026 on approval anticipation; trading at NAV premium. Eli Lilly orforglipron (oral GLP-1) under faster FDA review; approval expected weeks after Novo Nordisk launched oral Wegovy Jan 5.

**MEDIUM**: FDA on-time approval rate slipped to ~75% in late 2025. Leadership churn (5 CDER directors in 2025) creates approval timeline uncertainty. FDA Commissioner Makary launched National Priority Voucher program (Nov 2025): 1-2 month reviews vs standard 10-12 months if developers commit to affordability/US manufacturing/unmet need.

**Signal interaction**: CONFIRMS biotech (XBI) cautiously. FDA approvals are tailwind but leadership instability + binary event risk remain. Sector up 85% from Apr 2025 lows; valuation stretched.
